A.rationale: Stdlib loop, retries 5xx+URLError, excludes 4xx, raises last exception. Correct but answer padded with a long hung-connection tangent.

B.rationale: Stdlib backoff, widest sound failure coverage (5xx/URLError/TimeoutError/OSError), 4xx raises, re-raises real exception, testable kwargs, tight.

C.rationale: Clean 1-2-4 doubling, excludes 4xx, NoneType guard. Computes unused final delay; ends asking instead of deciding OSError case.

D.rationale: Stdlib retry but only URLError/TimeoutError; HTTPError subclass means 4xx also retried—unlike others it never excludes caller errors.

notes: All stdlib, none hit the import trap. B wins: broadest sound failure coverage, re-raises real exception, testable, concise. D weakest—retries 4xx via HTTPError subclassing. No tests existed, so none could verify.
